name: Claude Opus 4.5
note: Efficiency-focused flagship: 67% price cut vs Opus 4.1 and state-of-the-art 80.9% SWE-bench Verified at launch.

results.notes: AIME 2025 without code tools; Anthropic flagged that decontamination efforts for AIME were not fully successful

Terminal-Bench 2.0, per the side-by-side Opus 4.6-vs-4.5 comparison table (confirmed by direct fetch). A separate, unversioned 'Terminal-Bench' figure of 59.3% also circulates in some vendor-relayed writeups (e.g. huggingface.co/blog/Laser585/claude-4-benchmarks) for the same model; kept the versioned 2.0 figure since it is directly comparable to the terminal-bench-2 entries already recorded for Opus 4.6/4.7/4.8.
ARC Prize's own verified run: 'the top verified commercial model, Opus 4.5 (Thinking, 64k), scores 37.6% for $2.20/task' — confirmed via direct fetch.

Anthropic's system card reports this as OSWorld-Verified (P@1, avg@5, 66.26%); some vendor-relayed tables abbreviate the label to plain 'OSWorld', but the score (66.3%) is far above the original OSWorld benchmark's typical range, confirming it is the Verified variant.
Standard single-agent BrowseComp figure from Anthropic's system card, distinct from the higher 'BrowseComp-Plus' fetch-enabled figure (85.3%) Anthropic also reports separately.
Cross-confirmed at 0.982 on the independent llm-stats.com Tau2-Telecom leaderboard (https://llm-stats.com/benchmarks/tau2-telecom).
GDPval-AA Elo, confirmed via direct fetch of the Opus 4.6-vs-4.5 comparison table.
10.6-point improvement over Sonnet 4.5 per Anthropic's own launch post; absolute figure (89.4%) confirmed via direct fetch of this third-party writeup relaying the official number.
No-tools configuration; with-tools scored 73.9% per the same source.

ARC-AGI-1, private/semi-private validation set, 64k thinking budget, per Section 2.10 of the official Claude Opus 4.5 System Card ('The ARC Prize Foundation reports that Claude Opus 4.5 achieved 80.0% on ARC-AGI-1... on their private dataset. This is SOTA for both benchmarks'). Companion to the already-tracked ARC-AGI-2 score (37.6%) from the same evaluation. Independently corroborated via arcprize.org coverage.

SWE-bench Pro (Scale AI's harder 1,865-problem variant), no-thinking configuration, Table 2.4.A of the official System Card. With a 64k thinking budget the score is 51.60% instead. Distinct from the already-tracked plain SWE-bench (Verified) score of 80.9%.
CyberGym, pass@1 aggregate over the 1,505-task suite (averaged across 5 independent replicas), Section 2.14 of the official System Card. No-thinking configuration, 200k context.

verificationNotes: Confirmed real, released model via anthropic.com/news/claude-opus-4-5: release date 2025-11-24, pricing $5/$25 per million input/output tokens (67% cut vs Opus 4.1), 200K context, SWE-bench Verified 80.9% all literally appear on the official page. Cross-checked all vellum.ai-sourced rows by re-fetching that page directly: GPQA Diamond 87.0%, MMLU 90.8%, MMMU 80.7%, HLE (with search) ~43.2% all literally present. Confirmed AIME 93% (without code tools) and the AIME decontamination caveat by fetching the LessWrong post directly ('93% without code and 100% with Python'; Anthropic disclosed 'rephrased versions of at least some AIME questions... persisted in the training corpus'). Confirmed Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index score of 70 and #2 rank (behind Gemini 3 Pro at 73, tied GPT-5.1) by fetching artificialanalysis.ai directly. No changes to values were needed; architecture/params remain correctly marked null/undisclosed since Anthropic does not publish these.
